danger_signals.visual_form: Human enemies in dark robes/occult attire
danger_signals.meaning: Organized hostile faction; intellectual threat beyond simple monsters
Supernatural danger; divine/infernal opposition; world-state corruption

danger_signals.visual_form: Lava pits, crushing blocks, explosive barrels, spike traps
danger_signals.meaning: Level architecture itself is hostile; environmental puzzle-threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Bats, rats, eels, possessed hands appearing in groups
danger_signals.meaning: Low individual threat but overwhelming in numbers; attrition danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Demonic symbols, bloody textures, gothic architecture, corpses
danger_signals.meaning: Thematic danger signal; world is corrupted by dark forces

safety_signals.visual_form: Glowing items, distinctive weapon models (flare gun, voodoo doll, aerosol flamethrower)

safety_signals.meaning: Rewards for exploration; safe discovery spaces
Temporary invulnerability window; player advantage surge
Safe progression; escape from current danger zone

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Occult/demonic symbols associated with Tchernobog cult
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Human cultists in dark robes; members of 'The Chosen' (highest circle)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cult headquarters, ritual spaces, dark architecture infused with occult imagery

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Gargoyle imagery (Cheogh as primary minion); demonic sigils
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Gargoyles, demonic creatures, otherworldly entities serving the demon
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Futuristic/retro-futuristic technology mixing with gothic horror; anachronistic design suggesting otherworldly influence

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player character model; lone avenger archetype
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Weapons and gear from late 19th/early 20th century; Western motifs mixed with anachronistic technology

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environments dominated by cult architecture, occult symbols, dark gothic structures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cabal cultists patrol and defend territories; creatures serve Tchernobog actively

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Anachronistic mixing of 1920s-40s setting with futuristic/retro-futuristic technology and demonic elements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creatures fight for demon; environment contains magical hazards alongside mechanical traps

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Undead protagonist model; weapons spanning centuries; blend of Western gunslinger and modern/futuristic arms
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player actively hunts through levels seeking Cheogh and Tchernobog; progresses through increasingly corrupted spaces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Former allies (The Chosen members) absent; replaced by hostile Cabal members and demonic servants
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No neutral NPCs; all humanoid enemies are hostile; trust is visually absent

environmental_storytelling.example: 1920s-40s architecture, clothing, and vehicles coexist with futuristic technology and demonic/gothic elements, suggesting timeline corruption or Tchernobog's otherworldly influence warping reality
Demonic symbols, dark robes, gargoyle statuary, and ritual spaces communicate pervasive Cabal control and supernatural corruption throughout levels

Caleb's arsenal spans from late 19th century revolvers to bizarre modern/futuristic weapons (flare gun, voodoo doll, aerosol flamethrower), visually narrating his unnatural resurrection and time-displaced nature
Nuisance enemies (bats, rats, eels) are small and weak; Cabal cultists are humanoid and organized; demonic entities are large and grotesque—visual scale communicates threat tier
Traps (crushing blocks, lava pits, spikes) and explosive barrels show hostile architecture—the levels themselves resist intrusion, suggesting active defense mechanisms or corrupted spaces

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Use environment for cover; organized resistance; human-level tactical awareness
Aggressive assault; magical/supernatural attack patterns; serve demon's interests

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Nuisance enemies (bats, rats, eels, possessed hands)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Individually weak but dangerous in groups; low intelligence; harassment rather than tactical threat
Occupy key locations; represent ultimate threat and world-state corruption; require defeat to progress
Active progression through hostile levels; weapon acquisition; seeking specific targets (Cheogh, Tchernobog)

notes: Blood uses a deliberately anachronistic visual language to communicate world-state corruption: the mixing of 1920s-40s Americana with futuristic technology, Western gunslinger aesthetics with demonic/gothic imagery, and real historical periods with supernatural elements all reinforce that Tchernobog's influence has warped reality. The Cabal faction is visually distinguished through occult symbols and dark robes, while the player's undead nature is communicated through both character model and the strange diversity of their arsenal. Environmental storytelling is achieved primarily through architectural style, enemy type placement, and hazard design rather than explicit narrative markers. The game communicates danger through visual saturation of dark, corrupted spaces and safety through weapon/power-up placement and secret area rewards.
